title: Diplacus vandenbergensis
taxonomy: Diplacus vandenbergensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phrymaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as lopseed or loosestrife. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. As a member of the Phrymaceae family, it is closely related to other species such as Phryma leptostachya. The plant's classification has been determined through scientific research and analysis of its morphology and molecular biology. By understanding its classification, scientists can gain a deeper insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
